title: utricularia dichotoma subsp. novae-zelandiae
intro: Utricularia dichotoma subsp. novae-zelandiae, also known as the New Zealand bladderwort, is a carnivorous plant species native to New Zealand. This plant belongs to the genus Utricularia, characterized by its unique ability to capture and digest small aquatic organisms using bladder-like traps. New Zealand bladderwort has been found to possess medicinal properties, including antibacterial and anti-inflammatory compounds that can be used to treat various health issues. Herbal preparations of Utricularia dichotoma subsp. novae-zelandiae are typically made from its leaves and stems, which can be used to create teas, tinctures, and infusions that promote digestive health, reduce inflammation, and support immune function.
